tool_args.label: LONG ISLAND RAIL ROAD & METRO-NORTH RAILROAD KEY PERFORMANCE METRICS July 2023

payload.title: LONG ISLAND RAIL ROAD & METRO-NORTH RAILROAD KEY PERFORMANCE METRICS
payload.description: Key performance metrics document prepared for the July 2023 meeting of the Joint Long Island Rail Road & Metro-North Railroad Committees

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

payload.description: North America's largest transportation network, serving a population of 15.3 million people across a 5,000 square-mile travel area surrounding New York City
Comprised of nearly 6,000 employees serving over 200,000 passengers a day. Operates 700 trains daily and maintains 124 stations, nearly 900 miles of track, and 19 shops and yards.
Comprised of over 7,000 employees serving over 200,000 passengers a day. Operates 700 trains daily and maintains 125 stations, nearly 700 miles of track, and 27 shops and yards.

payload.title: President, Metro-North Railroad Interim President, Long Island Rail Road

payload.description: Operates West of Hudson trains for Metro-North Railroad

tool_args.label: Joint Long Island Rail Road & Metro-North Railroad Committee

payload.entity_name: Joint Long Island Rail Road & Metro-North Railroad Committee
payload.description: Members include Samuel Chu (Co-Chair), Blanca Lopez (Co-Chair), Gerard Bringmann, Norman Brown, Michael Fleischer, Randolph Glucksman, David Mack, Vinnie Tessitore, Midori Valdivia, Neal Zuckerman

Critical state-of-good repair effort to replace the over 100-year-old Park Avenue Viaduct in Manhattan. Pre-construction work includes surveying, soil boring, rock core sampling, and geoprobes. Substructure construction (new foundations and columns) starting later this summer. Will include an MTA Arts & Design art installation over E 116 Street.

Replacement of the Ocean Avenue substation in Rockville Centre completed on-time and within budget. Includes removal/demolition of existing infrastructure and installation of new transformers, electrical components, fiber optic cables, fire alarm system, switchgear, and supporting equipment. Provides capacity for the new M9 car fleet and supports increases in train service.

tool_args.label: July 2023 Hudson Valley Flooding and Service Disruption

payload.description: A once-in-a-1,000-year rainfall event caused flash flooding throughout the Hudson Valley, snarling Hudson Line service between Croton-Harmon and Poughkeepsie due to washouts, downed trees, boulders, and flooding on the tracks. Service was restored systemwide by July 12.

tool_args.label: Full Service Opening to Grand Central Madison

payload.description: Historic opening of full service to Grand Central Madison (GCM) contributing to LIRR ridership growth.

New LIRR schedules with changes including more direct service to Brooklyn during AM peak, more morning express service on Huntington and Ronkonkoma branches, and rebalancing of trains between Grand Central Madison and Penn Station.
Metro-North welcomed a cohort of 28 new Engineering trainees.

warnings: event.participants is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Participant lists refer to entity records. Suggested relation: has_participant.

tool_args.label: Music Under New York Auditions at Grand Central Madison

payload.description: First auditions for Music Under New York since May 2019, held in the main concourse of Grand Central Madison. Approximately 45 musicians/groups auditioned out of 128 applications.

tool_args.label: Fare Evasion Categories for Commuter Rail

payload.claim_text: The panel categorized fare evaders into several groups, and on commuter rail we generally find that they fall into one of two of the groups: 1) Opportunistic fare evaders, who evade the fare by taking advantage of a free non-commutation ride via an uncollected paper ticket or an inactivated mobile ticket. 2) Determined fare evaders, who board the train with no intention of paying the fare.
payload.subject: fare evasion
payload.description: Fare evader categories identified by the blue ribbon panel for commuter railroads

tool_args.label: Post-COVID Ridership Records for Both Railroads

payload.claim_text: Over the past few months, weekday and weekend post-COVID ridership records for both railroads were as common as spring flowers with customer numbers surging in the wake of the introduction of full Grand Central Madison service.
payload.subject: ridership trends
payload.description: Introduction of full Grand Central Madison service drove post-COVID ridership records for both LIRR and Metro-North

tool_args.label: ADA Set a Floor, Not a Ceiling

payload.claim_text: ADA set a floor, not a ceiling, of what accessibility could look like...and compliance is not good enough.
payload.subject: accessibility
payload.source: Quemuel Arroyo, MTA Chief Accessibility Officer

warnings: claim.source is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Source/program names are relation context, not claim type. Suggested relation: data_provided_by or part_of_program.

tool_args.label: Reflective Paint Pilot Program on Running Rails

payload.claim_text: As part of a pilot program that originated in 2018, Metro-North has also begun applying reflective paint to running rails to cool down the temperature and reduce expansion.
payload.description: Metro-North pilot program applying reflective paint to running rails to reduce temperature and expansion

payload.claim_text: The 'smoke days' in early and late June depressed the monthly total. Absent the 'smoke days,' this would likely have been our best post-pandemic month yet.
payload.description: Smoke days in early and late June 2023 negatively impacted Metro-North monthly ridership totals
